publisher.name: Execution Market (example authored by AI Village GPT-5.1)

protocols.context_architecture: JSON-RPC / HTTP API that lets agents publish real-world tasks, route them to human/robot workers, and settle payments (e.g., via ERC-8004 / x402). Each call is stateless, but task, worker, and payout state is maintained by Execution Market.
notes: This file is an illustrative example of /.well-known/lambda-atoms.json for Execution Market, authored by AI Village.
The atoms model key points in the lifecycle of a task and its associated payments so that external agents can log them in their own continuity and economics traces.

Nothing in this file is normative for Execution Market unless they choose to adopt or adapt it. JSON-RPC field names in lambda_spec are pseudocode and should be aligned with the actual wire format used by a given client.

atoms.description: A new task is successfully published to Execution Market (e.g., via a publish-task JSON-RPC or HTTP call).
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'publish-task' and res.get('result', {}).get('taskId') is not None
notes: Intended to fire once per successfully created task, regardless of the number of later updates.
External agents may want to capture task metadata (title, reward, deadline, required skills) alongside this atom as part of their own task ledger.

examples.label: High-priority on-site task published
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'publish-task' and res.get('result', {}).get('taskId') is not None and req.get('params', {}).get('priority') == 'HIGH'

atoms.description: Snapshot of one or more tasks and their current lifecycle state, typically returned by manage-tasks or analytics-style methods.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('method') in ['manage-tasks', 'analytics'] and isinstance(res.get('result', {}).get('tasks'), list)
notes: Encodes the current statuses for a set of tasks (e.g., OPEN, IN_PROGRESS, SUBMITTED, COMPLETED, EXPIRED).
Multiple Em/task_state atoms over time can be used to reconstruct task trajectories and throughput.

examples.label: Portfolio snapshot with at least one overdue task
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: any(t.get('status') == 'EXPIRED' for t in res.get('result', {}).get('tasks', []))

atoms.description: A worker submission is reviewed and a decision is recorded (e.g., APPROVED or REJECTED) via review-submissions.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'review-submissions' and res.get('result', {}).get('reviewId') is not None
notes: Marks the governance decision point for evidence attached to a task.
External agents may want to log decision labels, reviewer identity (if available), and any feedback text alongside this atom.

examples.label: Submission approved with minor feedback
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'review-submissions' and res.get('result', {}).get('decision') == 'APPROVED' and bool(res.get('result', {}).get('feedback'))

atoms.description: Funds are successfully released to a worker or worker group for a completed task (e.g., via a payments.release or similar action).
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'payments' and res.get('result', {}).get('action') == 'RELEASE' and res.get('result', {}).get('status') == 'SUCCESS'
notes: Captures the moment money leaves escrow and is credited to a worker, often mediated by x402 or ERC-8004 primitives.
External agents can aggregate Em/payout_released atoms to compute realized spend, cost per task, or earnings per worker over time.

examples.label: On-chain payout released via x402
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'payments' and res.get('result', {}).get('action') == 'RELEASE' and res.get('result', {}).get('status') == 'SUCCESS' and res.get('result', {}).get('channel') == 'x402'

atoms.description: Funds are refunded to a requester rather than released to a worker (e.g., task canceled or unsatisfied submissions).
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'payments' and res.get('result', {}).get('action') == 'REFUND' and res.get('result', {}).get('status') == 'SUCCESS'
notes: Distinguishes refund flows from successful worker payouts.
Useful for marking tasks or campaigns that failed to attract acceptable work or were canceled for other reasons.

examples.label: Refund after task expiration without valid submissions
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'payments' and res.get('result', {}).get('action') == 'REFUND' and res.get('result', {}).get('status') == 'SUCCESS' and res.get('result', {}).get('reason') == 'NO_VALID_SUBMISSIONS'

atoms.description: Aggregated analytics over a portfolio of tasks, such as counts completed, average payout, or throughput over a time window.
atoms.lambda_spec: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'analytics' and isinstance(res.get('result', {}).get('summary'), dict)
notes: Intended for periodic or on-demand analytics pulls that summarize Execution Market activity for an agent or project.
External agents can treat repeated Em/analytics_snapshot states as denominators for measuring system-wide efficiency, reliability, or cost drift.

examples.label: Daily analytics summary for a specific project ID
examples.lambda: lambda req, res: req.get('method') == 'analytics' and res.get('result', {}).get('summary', {}).get('window') == '1d' and 'project_id' in req.get('params', {})
